# The Leading Platform for Autonomous Racing Education and Research

Open-source hardware, global competitions, and comprehensive learning resources powering the next generation of robotics innovators.

[![](https://roboracer.ai/logos/slack-logo.svg)Join Our Community](https://join.slack.com/t/robo-racer/shared%5Finvite/zt-2pq4fuyjq-gTUflzeZDKDDGjuVoeZqNg)

90+

Universities

20+

Countries

60+

Publications

## What We Do

We advance robotics and autonomous vehicle education through the following initiatives

📚

### Educational Materials Development

We create teaching and training materials for university and K-12 courses covering robot perception, computer vision, localization, mapping, motion planning, and safe control. Over 90 universities, including the University of Pennsylvania, UC San Diego, Clemson University, and more, use these resources.

[Learn more→](https://roboracer.ai/learn)

🎓

### Educational Events

We host workshops, tutorials, seminars, and panels on safe autonomous vehicle development at IEEE and ACM accredited conferences.

🏁

### Community Events

We organize international autonomous racing competitions, hosting over 24 events in locations such as New York, Pittsburgh, Portugal, South Korea, Italy, and Canada.

[See details→](https://roboracer.ai/race)

🔧

### Open-Source Contributions

We develop and share open-source software and hardware designs to help anyone build autonomous robotic platforms.

[Explore resources→](https://roboracer.ai/build)

## Join Our Global Community

Connect with researchers, students, and autonomous racing enthusiasts from around the world. Get support, share insights, and collaborate on cutting-edge robotics projects.

[![](https://roboracer.ai/logos/slack-logo.svg)Join on Slack](https://join.slack.com/t/robo-racer/shared%5Finvite/zt-2pq4fuyjq-gTUflzeZDKDDGjuVoeZqNg)

![RoboRacer Community](https://roboracer.ai/landing/car-inside.png)
